title: Real-Time Transit Data Collection Loops
subtitle: A polling loop turns a live vehicle feed into an analyzable historical dataset.
summary: The Rutgers Bus Analysis project polled PassioGO every 30 seconds and collected hundreds of thousands of data points.

artifact.title: Repeated polling becomes a time series
artifact.description: The chart stands in for route observations accumulating across the day.

sections.heading: The Loop
body: A collector calls the API, timestamps the response, normalizes fields, writes records, waits, and repeats.
The important design detail is consistency: the same polling interval and schema make later analysis much easier.

sections.heading: Operational Concerns
body: Long-running collectors need retries, logs, disk checks, and a plan for API failures. A week of data is only useful if gaps are visible.

pitfalls: Assuming every vehicle reports at the same cadence.

quiz.question: Why timestamp each API response?
options: To reconstruct route behavior over time
quiz.explanation: Transit analysis depends on ordering observations in time.
